final-school-program-participation-repor.label: Final Capstone: School Program Participation Report
final-school-program-participation-repor.summary: Build one all-department participation report through a relationship-preview Try It and four cumulative capstone steps.

project.title: Final Capstone: School Program Participation Report

step2_add_counts.title: Step 2: Add Course and Enrollment Counts

step1_establish_grain.title: Step 1: Establish the All-Department Grain
step1_establish_grain.prompt: Start the final capstone project. In step 1 of 4, Start from the departments table and LEFT JOIN with courses and enrollments. Ensure that each result row represents one department.
step1_establish_grain.hint: This is the first project step. Build a clean starting version, then run it before moving on.

help.concept: The final capstone is progressive. Each step starts from the previous working solution and adds one focused feature.
help.hint_1: This is the first project step. Build a clean starting version, then run it before moving on.
help.hint_2: Use the role or evidence named in the prompt instead of relying on answer wording.

step1_establish_grain.solutionCode:
SELECT d.name AS department_name
FROM departments d
LEFT JOIN courses c ON d.id = c.department_id
LEFT JOIN enrollments e ON c.id = e.course_id
GROUP BY d.id;

step2_add_counts.title: Step 2: Add Course and Enrollment Counts
step2_add_counts.prompt: Continue the final capstone project from the previous working step. In step 2 of 4, Extend the previous query to include course_count and enrollment_count for each department.
step2_add_counts.hint: Begin with the working code from the previous step. Keep that code, follow the new comments, and add only the next focused behavior.

help.concept: The final capstone is progressive. Each step starts from the previous working solution and adds one focused feature.
help.hint_1: Begin with the working code from the previous step. Keep that code, follow the new comments, and add only the next focused behavior.
help.hint_2: Run the previous working code first, then make only the focused change for this step.

step2_add_counts.starterCode:
SELECT d.name AS department_name
FROM departments d
LEFT JOIN courses c ON d.id = c.department_id
LEFT JOIN enrollments e ON c.id = e.course_id
GROUP BY d.id;
-- Capstone step 2: Step 2: Add Course and Enrollment Counts
-- Keep the working code above from the previous step.
-- Next, Extend the previous query to include course_count and enrollment_count for each department
-- Add only the focused change for this step inside the existing work.
step2_add_counts.solutionCode:
SELECT d.name AS department_name, COUNT(c.id) AS course_count, COUNT(e.id) AS enrollment_count
FROM departments d
LEFT JOIN courses c ON d.id = c.department_id
LEFT JOIN enrollments e ON c.id = e.course_id
GROUP BY d.id;

main_py.content:
SELECT d.name AS department_name
FROM departments d
LEFT JOIN courses c ON d.id = c.department_id
LEFT JOIN enrollments e ON c.id = e.course_id
GROUP BY d.id;
-- Capstone step 2: Step 2: Add Course and Enrollment Counts
-- Keep the working code above from the previous step.
-- Next, Extend the previous query to include course_count and enrollment_count for each department
-- Add only the focused change for this step inside the existing work.

SELECT d.name AS department_name, COUNT(c.id) AS course_count, COUNT(e.id) AS enrollment_count
FROM departments d
LEFT JOIN courses c ON d.id = c.department_id
LEFT JOIN enrollments e ON c.id = e.course_id
GROUP BY d.id;

step3_add_distinct_students.title: Step 3: Add Distinct Student Participation
step3_add_distinct_students.prompt: Continue the final capstone project from the previous working step. In step 3 of 4, Modify the query to include a count of distinct students participating in each department.
step3_add_distinct_students.hint: Begin with the working code from the previous step. Keep that code, follow the new comments, and add only the next focused behavior.

help.concept: The final capstone is progressive. Each step starts from the previous working solution and adds one focused feature.
help.hint_1: Begin with the working code from the previous step. Keep that code, follow the new comments, and add only the next focused behavior.
help.hint_2: Run the previous working code first, then make only the focused change for this step.

step3_add_distinct_students.starterCode:
SELECT d.name AS department_name, COUNT(c.id) AS course_count, COUNT(e.id) AS enrollment_count
FROM departments d
LEFT JOIN courses c ON d.id = c.department_id
LEFT JOIN enrollments e ON c.id = e.course_id
GROUP BY d.id;
-- Capstone step 3: Step 3: Add Distinct Student Participation
-- Keep the working code above from the previous step.
-- Next, Modify the query to include a count of distinct students participating in each department
-- Add only the focused change for this step inside the existing work.
step3_add_distinct_students.solutionCode:
SELECT d.name AS department_name, COUNT(c.id) AS course_count, COUNT(e.id) AS enrollment_count, COUNT(DISTINCT e.student_id) AS student_count
FROM departments d
LEFT JOIN courses c ON d.id = c.department_id
LEFT JOIN enrollments e ON c.id = e.course_id
GROUP BY d.id;

main_py.content:
SELECT d.name AS department_name, COUNT(c.id) AS course_count, COUNT(e.id) AS enrollment_count
FROM departments d
LEFT JOIN courses c ON d.id = c.department_id
LEFT JOIN enrollments e ON c.id = e.course_id
GROUP BY d.id;
-- Capstone step 3: Step 3: Add Distinct Student Participation
-- Keep the working code above from the previous step.
-- Next, Modify the query to include a count of distinct students participating in each department
-- Add only the focused change for this step inside the existing work.

SELECT d.name AS department_name, COUNT(c.id) AS course_count, COUNT(e.id) AS enrollment_count, COUNT(DISTINCT e.student_id) AS student_count
FROM departments d
LEFT JOIN courses c ON d.id = c.department_id
LEFT JOIN enrollments e ON c.id = e.course_id
GROUP BY d.id;

step4_label_rank.title: Step 4: Label and Rank Participation
step4_label_rank.prompt: Continue the final capstone project from the previous working step. In step 4 of 4, Add a participation_status column and sort the results by enrollment_count descending and department_name ascending.
step4_label_rank.hint: Begin with the working code from the previous step. Keep that code, follow the new comments, and add only the next focused behavior.

help.concept: The final capstone is progressive. Each step starts from the previous working solution and adds one focused feature.
help.hint_1: Begin with the working code from the previous step. Keep that code, follow the new comments, and add only the next focused behavior.
help.hint_2: Run the previous working code first, then make only the focused change for this step.

step4_label_rank.starterCode:
SELECT d.name AS department_name, COUNT(c.id) AS course_count, COUNT(e.id) AS enrollment_count, COUNT(DISTINCT e.student_id) AS student_count
FROM departments d
LEFT JOIN courses c ON d.id = c.department_id
LEFT JOIN enrollments e ON c.id = e.course_id
GROUP BY d.id;
-- Capstone step 4: Step 4: Label and Rank Participation
-- Keep the working code above from the previous step.
-- Next, Add a participation_status column and sort the results by enrollment_count descending and department_name ascending
-- Add only the focused change for this step inside the existing work.
step4_label_rank.solutionCode:
SELECT d.name AS department_name, COUNT(c.id) AS course_count, COUNT(e.id) AS enrollment_count, COUNT(DISTINCT e.student_id) AS student_count,
CASE
  WHEN COUNT(e.id) = 0 THEN 'No activity'
  WHEN COUNT(c.id) >= 2 AND COUNT(DISTINCT e.student_id) >= 2 THEN 'Broad participation'
  ELSE 'Active'
END AS participation_status
FROM departments d
LEFT JOIN courses c ON d.id = c.department_id
LEFT JOIN enrollments e ON c.id = e.course_id
GROUP BY d.id
ORDER BY enrollment_count DESC, department_name ASC;

main_py.content:
SELECT d.name AS department_name, COUNT(c.id) AS course_count, COUNT(e.id) AS enrollment_count, COUNT(DISTINCT e.student_id) AS student_count
FROM departments d
LEFT JOIN courses c ON d.id = c.department_id
LEFT JOIN enrollments e ON c.id = e.course_id
GROUP BY d.id;
-- Capstone step 4: Step 4: Label and Rank Participation
-- Keep the working code above from the previous step.
-- Next, Add a participation_status column and sort the results by enrollment_count descending and department_name ascending
-- Add only the focused change for this step inside the existing work.

SELECT d.name AS department_name, COUNT(c.id) AS course_count, COUNT(e.id) AS enrollment_count, COUNT(DISTINCT e.student_id) AS student_count,
CASE
  WHEN COUNT(e.id) = 0 THEN 'No activity'
  WHEN COUNT(c.id) >= 2 AND COUNT(DISTINCT e.student_id) >= 2 THEN 'Broad participation'
  ELSE 'Active'
END AS participation_status
FROM departments d
LEFT JOIN courses c ON d.id = c.department_id
LEFT JOIN enrollments e ON c.id = e.course_id
GROUP BY d.id
ORDER BY enrollment_count DESC, department_name ASC;

sketch-intro.title: Introduction to the Capstone Project
sketch-intro.bodyMarkdown: In this final capstone project, you will create a comprehensive report that shows participation across all school departments. The goal is to ensure that every department is represented, even those without active courses. You'll use SQL to join tables, calculate metrics, and label participation levels. This project will help you consolidate your SQL skills and produce a portfolio-quality report.
